task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly implements the trimmed mean algorithm, but could be more descriptive about its edge cases.
trace: In the provided code, we first handle the edge case of an empty list by returning None. For a single-element list, we return the value directly as it is equivalent to the mean in this case. For larger lists, we sort them and then calculate the indices of the minimum and maximum occurrences based on the trimmed mean formula (dropping one minimum and one maximum). Finally, we calculate the mean of the remaining numbers and return it as a float.
